compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
`_acquire_fcntl` (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py:127-140`) catches EVERY `OSError` from
`fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)` identically: it sleeps and ultimately returns the timeout
sentinel `-1`. So a genuine system fault (ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF) is indistinguishable from real
contention (EAGAIN/EACCES) and gets masked as a spurious 30-60s `LockTimeout`. The mkdir leg
already branches on `errno` (`lock.py:222-230`). VALIDATED: no direct test of
`_acquire_fcntl`.

Note on EINTR: under PEP 475 (CPython ≥3.5) the interpreter retries a syscall interrupted by
a signal (including `fcntl.flock`) at the C level before returning to Python, so
`OSError(EINTR)` does not surface in user code — there is deliberately NO EINTR branch here
(it would be unreachable dead code).

## Caller-impact analysis
`_acquire_fcntl` has one caller — `acquire` (`lock.py:288`), which maps `fd < 0 → raise
LockTimeout`. `LockTimeout` is caught at ~12 sites (inbound_translate, sync, event_append×2,
ensures, push, compact×2, txn, fsck, _seam) — but every one handles it as **contention** (a
lock not acquired within the budget). This change leaves the contention path UNCHANGED
(EAGAIN/EACCES still → `-1` → `LockTimeout`), so all 12 handlers behave exactly as before.
The only behavioral change: a genuine unexpected fault (ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF) now propagates as
`OSError` (with its errno) instead of being masked as a spurious `LockTimeout` — which is the
bug being fixed. No handler wants to swallow a real EIO/ENOLCK as a lock timeout; failing
loud on a true fault is strictly more correct.

## What
Branch `_acquire_fcntl` on `errno`:
- **EAGAIN / EACCES (contention):** retry until `deadline`, then close the fd and return
  `-1` (unchanged).
- **Any other errno (ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF/…):** close the fd and re-raise the `OSError`
  immediately, preserving its errno/identity.
The fd opened at the top is closed on EVERY non-success exit (the timeout return AND the
re-raise path); on success the caller owns and closes it (unchanged).

Add direct tests of `_acquire_fcntl` that inject specific errnos by monkeypatching the
module's `fcntl.flock`.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` — errno branching in `_acquire_fcntl`; close fd on all paths.
- `tests/unit/test_lock_stale_reclaim.py` — new direct `_acquire_fcntl` errno tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EAGAIN/EACCES: retries until the deadline, then returns `-1` (contention behavior
      unchanged) — asserted with `flock` always raising EAGAIN and a near-immediate deadline.
- [ ] A non-contention errno (e.g. ENOLCK) is re-raised immediately as `OSError` with that
      errno (not masked as `-1`/`LockTimeout`) — asserted with `flock` raising ENOLCK, and
      confirming it raises promptly (before the deadline), not after waiting.
- [ ] The fd is closed on every failure path (the `-1` timeout return AND the re-raise) —
      asserted by spying on the module's `os.close` and confirming the opened fd was closed.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: branch _acquire_fcntl on errno (contention vs unexpected); close fd on all paths

compiled_state.title: Discriminate contention from unexpected errors in _acquire_fcntl
